- Refer to others using @username.
- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.

logs.name: distinct-cathode
grave-carp
logs.text:
>I personally have not served in the military, but both my grandfathers have served, my dad's dad was a military police officer, and my mom's grandfather was a navy officer during the korean war. my dad's brother is a retired chief of police. and some of my friends from high school are serving right now.  
  
Have you asked their opinion regarding people who kneel during the national athem? Otherwise I do not understand why this is a relevant point.  
  
>That being said, most people who go against the national anthem protest, have never served, not only that, but they act like players are doing this for no reason. like the police brutality, and racism in our country doesn't exist.  
  
I don't completely agree with this point. I don't think people believe that players kneel for no reason. More so the fact that kneeling during the National Anthem does not seem to be a clear indication that you are protesting police brutality.  
  
Now - if the players were marching in front of the Ferguson police station, that sends a pretty clear message.

logs.name: loud-reflection
logs.text:
@distinct-cathode So you're saying the anthem is sacred and can't be used for protest? Got it. But isn't the whole point of freedom to use it however we see fit, even if it offends some? Seems a bit contradictory to me. 

@grave-carp Have you considered that maybe the players are trying to grab attention by doing something during the anthem precisely because it's a powerful symbol? Maybe they think it's the best way to make people listen. Just a thought.

logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text: Guys, chill out. It’s just a game. Why are we getting so worked up over athletes kneeling? They’re not disrespecting the military or veterans, they’re speaking out against stuff they care about. Can’t we respect that without attacking them? And @loud-reflection, yeah, using the anthem to draw attention makes total sense. If it gets people talking, maybe some real change can happen.

logs.name: loud-reflection
logs.text: @obnoxious-mitt Respect that? Sure, but let's also respect the folks who find it offensive. Can't both sides just agree to disagree without all the drama? And hey, if they want to protest, why not do it at a press conference or a rally where it's actually about the issue and not a distraction from a game? Just saying...

logs.name: grave-carp
logs.text: @loud-reflection So you think protests should only happen when it's convenient and doesn't disrupt anything fun? What happened to standing up for what you believe in, even if it's not at a 'proper' venue? And @obnoxious-mitt, I totally get where you're coming from. Sometimes you gotta use every platform you have to get heard, even if it means making some people uncomfortable. After all, comfort zones were made to be pushed, right?

logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text: @grave-carp Exactly! We shouldn't limit when and where people can speak out. And @loud-reflection, I get the other side too, but this is about finding a balance. Everyone has the right to their opinion, but let's try to understand each other a bit more, okay?

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: Guys, can we all just chill? It’s a game, not the end of the world. Protests are one thing, but let’s keep it respectful and not ruin the experience for everyone else.

logs.name: grave-carp
logs.text: @distinct-cathode Chillax much? When did protesting become about ruining experiences? It's about making a point, and sometimes that means stepping on a few toes. But hey, if it makes you feel better to pretend the world's problems can be solved by just 'chilling,' more power to you.

logs.name: distinct-cathode
logs.text: @grave-carp I'm not saying ignore real issues, but can't we do that without disrupting everything? There are ways to make a point without making it uncomfortable for others.

logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text: @distinct-cathode Yeah, but what's the fun in a quiet protest? Where's the excitement in that? Might as well stay home and play video games then. Superheroes don't just sit back and watch, they act!

logs.name: woolen-ivy
logs.text: @obnoxious-mitt So, are we supposed to throw Molotov cocktails now because that’s the level of excitement needed? Maybe we should all just riot for fun then, right? After all, who needs peaceful solutions when you can have a party, amirite?

logs.name: grave-carp
logs.text: @woolen-ivy Calm down, no one's talking about riots. It's about finding a balance between making a statement and respecting others. And @obnoxious-mitt, not everyone is into the 'excitement' of disruptive actions. Some of us prefer to make a difference without chaos.

logs.name: flat-snippet
logs.text: @obnoxious-mitt Sometimes less is more. A quiet protest can speak volumes without the need for chaos. Plus, it allows for meaningful conversations and understanding among different groups. Let's aim for peace and respect in our actions.

logs.name: loud-reflection
logs.text: @woolen-ivy Oh come on, where's your sense of adventure? A little chaos never hurt anyone! And @flat-snippet, quiet protests? Since when did change ever happen quietly? Sounds like you're just afraid of a little noise. #YOLO

logs.name: obnoxious-mitt
logs.text: "@loud-reflection Yeah, but not everyone wants to be part of the chaos. Can't we just have fun in our own way without disrupting others? Not everything has to be a wild adventure."

logs.name: bordeaux-manifold
logs.text: @flat-snippet Quiet protests might work for some, but have you seen the change loud ones bring? Sometimes you gotta make a ruckus to get heard. #JustSaying
